questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: One mole of an ideal monoatomic gas is carried through a cyclic process as shown in the $\mathbf{P}$ vs V plot. Identify the correct expressions
questions.solution:
Work done along $\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\mathbf{0}(\because \mathrm{V}$ is constant $)$
Heat gained along $A \rightarrow B, Q_{A B}=n C v \Delta T$
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{3 R}{2}(400-250) \\
& =225 R
\end{aligned}$

Work done along $\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{AC}}=\mathrm{nR} \Delta \mathrm{T}$
$=R(150-250)=-100 R$
$\Delta \mathrm{E}$ along $\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}, \Delta \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{AC}}=\mathrm{nCv} \mathrm{\Delta T}$
$=\frac{3 R}{2}(150-250)=-150 R$

Work done along $\mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{BC}} \neq 0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q:
2 moles of an ideal monoatomic gas is taken from state A to state B through a process AB in which $\mathrm{PT}=$ constant. The process can be represented on a P-T graph as :
Identify the correct statement(s).
options: Heat evolved by the gas during the process $A B=-2100 R$
Heat absorbed by the gas during the process $A B=+2100 R$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A 2 mole Ne gas sample in a 30 L container fitted with frictionless piston is expanding adiabatically reversibly to 60 L at initial temperature 400 K . Which of the following is/are correct statement(s)?
options: Work done in the above process is around - 1867.5 kJ
Change in temperature is of 150 K (approx.)
The initial pressure is 2.2 atm and final pressure is 0.68 atm
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The normal boiling point of a liquid $(A)$ is $350 \mathrm{~K} . \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\text {vap }}$ at normal boiling point is $35 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. Pick out the correct statement(s). [Assume $\Delta \mathbf{H}_{\text {vap }}$ is independent of pressure]
questions.solution:
$\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\text {vap }}$ at 350 K and 1 atm
$=\frac{35 \times 1000}{350}=100 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
$\begin{aligned} &\left(\Delta S_{\text {vap }}\right)_{0.5 \mathrm{~atm}}=\left(\Delta S_{\mathrm{vap}}\right)_{1 \mathrm{~atm}}+1 \times R \ln \frac{1}{0.5} \\ &\gt100 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \\ &\left(\Delta \mathrm{~S}_{\mathrm{vap}}\right)_{2 \mathrm{~atm}}=\left(\Delta \mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{vap}}\right)_{1 \mathrm{~atm}}+R \ln \frac{1}{2} \lt 100 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \\ &\left(\Delta \mathrm{G}_{\mathrm{vap}}\right)_{2 \mathrm{~atm}}=\Delta \mathrm{H}-\mathrm{T}(\Delta \mathrm{S})_{2 \mathrm{~atm}}\gt0 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: On the basis of the following graph $(\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{V})$ graph, choose the correct statements.
options: The entropy change for the over all process is zero
questions.solution: Thus it is a cyclic process. Hence, $\Delta E=0, \Delta H=0, \Delta S=0$ and $\begin{gathered}\Delta \mathrm{E}=\mathrm{q}+\mathrm{w}\left(1^{\text {st }} \text { law }\right) \\ \therefore 0=\mathrm{q}+\mathrm{w}\end{gathered}$ or $q=-w$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { Total work done }=\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{B}}+\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}}+\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{A}} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{w}=-\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{B}}-\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{A}}\right)+0+2.303 \mathrm{nRT} \log \frac{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{C}}}{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{A}}} \\ & =-(40-20)+0+2.303 \times 1 \times 0.082 \times \log \frac{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{C}}}{\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{A}}} \\ & =-6.13 \text { litre-atmosphere } \\ & =-620.77 \mathrm{~J}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: The oxidation number of $\mathrm{Cr}$ in $\mathrm{CrO}_{5}$ is +6
$\Delta H>\Delta U$ for the reaction $N_{2} O_{4}(g) \rightarrow 2 N O_{2}(g)$ provided both gases behave ideally.
$\mathrm{pH}$ of $0.1 \mathrm{N} \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$ is less than that of $0.1 \mathrm{~N~} \mathrm{HC}$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
questions.solution: In $\mathrm{CrO}_{5}$ oxidation no. of $\mathrm{Cr}$ is +6. (b) For the reaction (in ideal case) $\begin{array}{r} \mathrm{N}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{4}(g) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NO}_{2}(g) \\ \Delta H=\Delta U+p \cdot \Delta V \end{array}$ $\because \quad \Delta H=\Delta U+\Delta n_{g} R T$ $\left(\because p \Delta V=\Delta n_{g} R T\right)$ Where, $\Delta n_{g}=$ (gaseous moles of product)-(gaseous moles of reactant) $\Delta n_{g}=2-1=1$ Thus, $\Delta H>\Delta U$ (c) ${p H}$ of $0.1 \mathrm{N} \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$ is less than of $0.1 \mathrm{~N~HCl}$ at $25^{\circ}$ is a wrong statement. $\mathrm{pH}$ of $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$ $\because \mathrm{pH}=-\log \left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=-\log \left[\mathrm{l} 0^{-2}\right]$ $\because \quad(N=C \times Z)$ and $\left(Z=2\right.$ for $\left.\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}\right)$ $\mathrm{pH}=2$ and $\mathrm{pH}$ of $\mathrm{HCl}$ $\mathrm{pH}=-\log \left[\mathrm{l} 0^{-1}\right]=1$ Thus, $\mathrm{pH}$ of $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}>\mathrm{pH}$ of $\mathrm{HCl}$. (d) $\frac{R T}{F}=0.0591$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is a wrong statement. Where, $R=$ Gas constant $=8.314$ $T=$ Temperature $=273+25=298 \mathrm{K}$ $F=$ Charge over one mole of electrons $=96500 \mathrm{C}$ Thus, $\frac{R T}{F}=\frac{8.314 \times 298}{96500}=0.02567$ The correct value is $\frac{2.303 R T}{F}=0.0591$ Hence, only option (a) and (b) are correct.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For a spontaneous process, the correct statement(s) is (are)
questions.solution:
For a process to be spontaneous.
$\begin{aligned} \Delta S_{\text {system }}+\Delta S_{\text {surrounding }} &>0 \\ &\left(\Delta G_{\text {system }}\right)_{T, p} < 0 \\ &\left(\Delta U_{\text {system }}\right)_{T, V} < 0 \end{aligned}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Among the following, the extensive variables
are
questions.solution:
Among the given options, the e
xtensive variables are $H$ (enthalpy), $E$ (internal energy). $V$ (volume).These variables have values that depends upon the quantity or size of matter present in the system. Other examples are heat
capacity, entropy, free energy, length and mass.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For spontaneous polymerization, which of the following is (are) correct?
questions.solution:
Hint:
$\Delta \mathrm{G}$ is negative as process is spontaneous.
$\Delta \mathrm{S}$ is negative due to association.
Therefore to make $\Delta$ G negative, $\Delta H$ must be negative.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
